back_translation: And the Philistines gathered their camps for war and assembled at Socoh of Judah, and they camped between Socoh and Azekah at Ephes-dammim.
And Saul and the men of Israel gathered and camped in the Valley of Elah, and they arrayed for battle to meet the Philistines.
And the Philistines stood on the hill on one side, and Israel stood on the hill on the other side, and the valley was between them.
And a champion went out from the camps of the Philistines — Goliath was his name, from Gath. His height was six cubits and a span.
And he had a bronze helmet on his head, and he wore scale armor, and the weight of the armor was five thousand shekels of bronze.
And he had bronze greaves on his legs, and a bronze javelin between his shoulders.
And the shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam, and the blade of his spear was six hundred shekels of iron, and the shield-bearer walked before him.

And he stood and called out to the ranks of Israel and said to them, 'Why do you come out to array for battle? Am I not a Philistine and you servants of Saul? Choose for yourselves a man and let him come down to me.
If he is able to fight with me and strike me, we will become your servants. But if I am able for him and strike him, you will become our servants and serve us.'
And the Philistine said, 'I have defied the ranks of Israel this day. Give me a man, and let us fight together.'
And Saul and all Israel heard the words of this Philistine, and they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
And David was the son of an Ephrathite man from Bethlehem of Judah, and his name was Jesse, and he had eight sons. And the man in the days of Saul was old, advanced in years.
And the three older sons of Jesse went after Saul to the battle, and the names of his three sons who went into battle were Eliab the firstborn, his second Abinadab, and the third Shammah.
And David was the youngest, and the three oldest had followed Saul.
And David went back and forth from Saul to tend the sheep of his father in Bethlehem.
And the Philistine drew near morning and evening, and presented himself for forty days.
And Jesse said to David his son, 'Take please for your brothers an ephah of this roasted grain and these ten loaves, and run to the camp to your brothers.
And these ten portions of cheese bring to the commander of the thousand, and check on your brothers for welfare, and bring back their pledge.'
And Saul and they and all the men of Israel were in the Valley of Elah fighting against the Philistines.

And David rose early in the morning and left the flock with a keeper, and lifted up and went as Jesse had commanded him. And he came to the camp as the army was going out to the battle line and shouting in battle.
And Israel and the Philistines arrayed line against line.
And David left his supplies in the hand of the keeper of supplies, and ran to the battle line and came and asked his brothers concerning welfare.
And while he was speaking with them, behold, the champion came up — Goliath the Philistine was his name, from Gath — from the ranks of the Philistines. And he spoke as these words, and David heard.
And all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from his face and feared greatly.

And the men of Israel said, 'Have you seen this man who comes up? For he comes up to defy Israel. And the man who strikes him — the king will enrich him with great wealth and will give to him his daughter, and will make his father's house free in Israel.'

And David said to the men who stood with him, saying, 'What will be done for the man who strikes this Philistine and removes shame from upon Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should defy the ranks of the living God?'
And the people said to him as this word, saying, 'Thus will be done for the man who strikes him.'

And Eliab his older brother heard him speaking to the men, and Eliab's anger burned against David, and he said, 'Why have you come down here? And with whom did you leave those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your insolence and the wickedness of your heart, for you have come down to see the battle.'
And David said, 'What have I done now? Was it not just a word?'
And he turned from beside him to face another and said as this word, and the people answered him a word as the first word.
And the words that David spoke were heard, and they reported them before Saul, and he took him.
And David said to Saul, 'Let no man's heart fall upon him. Your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.'
And Saul said to David, 'You are not able to go to this Philistine to fight with him, for you are a boy and he is a man of war from his youth.'
And David said to Saul, 'Your servant was a shepherd for his father with the flock, and a lion came and a bear and carried off a lamb from the flock.
And I went out after him and struck him and rescued from his mouth. And he rose against me, and I grabbed him by his beard and struck him and killed him.
Both the lion and the bear your servant struck, and this uncircumcised Philistine will be like one of them, for he has defied the ranks of the living God.'

And David said, 'The LORD who rescued me from the hand of the lion and from the hand of the bear — He will rescue me from the hand of this Philistine.' And Saul said to David, 'Go, and the LORD be with you.'
And Saul clothed David in his garments and put a bronze helmet on his head, and clothed him in armor.

And David girded his sword over his garments and tried to go, for he had not tested them. And David said to Saul, 'I cannot go in these, for I have not tested them.' And David removed them from upon him.

And he took his staff in his hand, and chose for himself five smooth stones from the brook and put them in the vessel of the shepherds that he had, and in the pouch — and his sling was in his hand. And he drew near to the Philistine.
And the Philistine walked, walking and drawing near to David, and the man bearing the shield was before him.
And the Philistine looked and saw David and despised him, for he was a boy, ruddy with handsome appearance.
And the Philistine said to David, 'Am I a dog, that you come to me with sticks?' And the Philistine cursed David by his gods.
And the Philistine said to David, 'Come to me, and I will give your flesh to the birds of the heavens and to the beasts of the field.'
And David said to the Philistine, 'You come to me with sword and with spear and with javelin, but I come to you in the name of the LORD of Hosts, the God of the ranks of Israel whom you have defied.

This day the LORD will deliver you into my hand, and I will strike you and remove your head from upon you, and I will give the corpses of the Philistine camp this day to the birds of the heavens and to the beasts of the earth, and all the earth will know that there is a God for Israel.
And all this assembly will know that not by sword or by spear does the LORD save, for the battle is the LORD's, and He will give you into our hand.'
And it happened, when the Philistine arose and walked and drew near to meet David, that David hastened and ran to the battle line to meet the Philistine.

And David sent his hand into the vessel, and took from there a stone, and slung it, and struck the Philistine on his forehead, and the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell upon his face to the ground.
And David prevailed over the Philistine with the sling and with the stone, and struck the Philistine and put him to death — and no sword was in David's hand.

And David ran and stood over the Philistine, and took his sword and drew it out from its sheath, and put him to death, and cut off with it his head. And the Philistines saw that their hero was dead, and they fled.

And the men of Israel and Judah arose and shouted and pursued the Philistines as far as the entrance of the valley and up to the gates of Ekron, and the slain of the Philistines fell along the way of Shaaraim, and up to Gath and up to Ekron.
And the children of Israel returned from pursuing after the Philistines, and they plundered their camps.
And David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem, and his weapons he put in his tent.
And when Saul saw David going out to meet the Philistine, he said to Abner the commander of the army, 'Whose son is this youth, Abner?' And Abner said, 'As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.'
And the king said, 'You inquire whose son this young man is.'
And when David returned from striking the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul, and the head of the Philistine was in his hand.
And Saul said to him, 'Whose son are you, youth?' And David said, 'The son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.'
